commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: There. Septuagint, "O that an umpire, (or mediator) were between us, and one arguing and giving ear in the midst of both!"

commentaries.author: John Chrysostom
commentaries.content: This is what he means, if he who punishes were a man, his punishment would not have entirely condemned the one in affliction. I could have been judged before him and proved that he too is unjust. But since you are God, that is impossible. It is sufficient to be punished and to suffer the greatest condemnation. - "Commentary on Job 9.32a"
